title: Arbiter of Steel — Aftermath
description: The Arbiter kneels with deliberate dignity. Her assessment of the party is blunt, precise, and carries unexpected warmth. She points them to the sealed passage. The sword knows they are real.

lines: The Arbiter fell, and the kneeling was deliberate — the motion of something that has chosen its posture.
The forest clearing was very still. The corruption around its edges seemed to wait.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I asked for your best and you gave it. I didn't expect to like being beaten this much.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: It is not a compliment. It is a fact.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: Your movements are irregular. Not trained. Not disciplined. But you persist through mistakes in a way that suggests you haven't yet accepted their cost.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: That is either a flaw or a quality. I cannot determine which.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: That answer is outside my assessment criteria.

steps.text: The Arbiter was quiet for a moment, and the quiet had weight. She looked at the Fighter's sword.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: Whatever you are — the sword knows you're real.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: Behind the sealed passage to the north. The court magic there was not intended to be found.

steps.text: She rose. Behind her, a sealed passage. Carved into the seal, in the same precise hand as the cave: The crown is not what it seems.
